name: Devilish Raspberry Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 cups nonfat plain or vanilla yogurt
recipeInstructions:
Cake:
1. In a large bowl, thoroughly mix the whole wheat and unbleached flours, brown sugar, cocoa, baking powder, and baking soda.
2. In another large bowl, combine the yogurt, oil, egg substitute, and vanilla. Beat for about 30 seconds to combine.
3. Add the dry ingredients and beat on low speed until just combined. Increase speed to high and beat for 3 minutes.
4. Coat a 9" x 9" (inches) baking dish or a 9" springform pan with non-stick spray. If desired, cut a piece of wax paper to fit the bottom and spray it. Bake at 350 degrees F for 35 to 45 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
5. Let cool on a wire rack. If unmolding, let cool on the rack for 10 minutes, then unmold onto a plate and peel off the paper. Cool completely.
Raspberry Sauce:
1. Puree 1 cup of raspberries and set aside.
2. In a 2-quart saucepan over medium heat, melt the preserves. Stir in the lemon juice and pureed raspberries. Heat for one minute. Add the remaining 2 cups of raspberries and stir well to coat them with glaze. Set aside to cool.
Whipped Topping (optional):
1. In a small bowl, combine the Just Whites and water (equivalent of 1 egg white). Whip until dissolved and foamy.
2. Add 1/16 tsp cream of tartar and whip until soft peaks form.
3. Gradually add 2 tbsp sugar and beat until stiff.
4. In another bowl, whip the whipping cream and vanilla until it forms soft peaks. Fold in the meringue.
Serving:
Cut the cake into pieces and transfer to dessert plates. Spoon sauce over the cake and add whipped topping if desired. Makes 12 servings. Per serving: 261 calories, 10 g fat (34% of calories).
